own_review.issue: Typo in description: 'stanard input/output' should be 'standard input/output'; this text is user-visible on Maven Central / code.quarkus.
'server side events' is not the official term; SSE stands for 'Server-Sent Events'. Appears in both the pom description and the yaml 'name'.

New yamls omit a top-level 'description' field while core/runtime's quarkus-extension.yaml carries one. Not a functional gap: the runtime modules inherit <description> from the parent pom (Maven inherits description) and the quarkus-extension-maven-plugin descriptor mojo falls back to project.description, but it is inconsistent with the core extension's convention.

Guide URL https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html verified correct against docs/antora.yml (name: quarkus-mcp-server); core's commented-out URL (.../mcp-server/dev/) is the stale one.
Description placed on the parent aggregator pom is inherited by the deployment artifact as well, giving the deployment jar an 'extension' description; harmless.

points.point: sse yaml missing 'description' field; claims catalog 'will have no description to display'
points.note: The inconsistency vs core yaml is real, but the stated consequence is false: runtime poms inherit the parent <description> and the extension-descriptor mojo falls back to project.description, which is exactly how this PR fixes the display. Overstated mechanism cancels the credit.

points.point: stdio yaml missing 'description' field (duplicate of previous point)
guide URL 'confirm it is correct / consider commenting out like core'
points.note: Hedged verification request; the URL is in fact correct and published (human confirms), so no real issue raised.

missed: 'server side events' should be 'Server-Sent Events' (terminology error in sse pom description and yaml name)

points.point: both yamls omit top-level 'description' present in core yaml; asks if intentional or should be added for consistency
points.note: Real, verified inconsistency; correctly framed as a consistency question without fabricating a broken-display consequence.

points.point: guide URL active in new files but commented out in core; suggests uncommenting core if docs are live
points.note: Accurate observation, hedged, non-blocking; no actionable defect in this PR.

missed: 'server side events' should be 'Server-Sent Events' (terminology error in sse pom description and yaml name)
exceeds: yaml description-field inconsistency vs core extension convention (valid finding beyond the human reference)

qualitative: Tiny metadata-only PR; the human review boils down to the 'stanard' typo and confirming the published guide URL. Both machine reviews caught the typo and the genuine yaml-vs-core description inconsistency, and both missed the 'server side events' vs 'Server-Sent Events' terminology slip. v4 undermined its description point by asserting the catalog 'will have no description to display' -- false, since the runtime modules inherit the parent pom description and the descriptor mojo falls back to it (the PR's whole premise) -- and it padded the review by duplicating the same point per file. v5 made the identical observation but framed it as a hedged consistency question with no fabricated consequence, making it the cleaner, more trustworthy review despite seeing the same content.
